title: Fix manual detach from containers to not wait for exit
body:
At present, when manually detaching from an attached container (using the detach hotkeys, default C-p C-q), Podman will still wait for the container to exit to obtain its exit code (so we can set Podman's exit code to match). This is correct in the case where attach finished because the container exited, but very wrong for the manual detach case.

As a result of this, we can no longer guarantee that the cleanup and --rm functions will fire at the end of 'podman run' - we may be exiting before we get that far. Cleanup is easy enough - we swap to unconditionally using the cleanup processes we've used for detached and rootless containers all along. To duplicate --rm we need to also teach 'podman cleanup' to optionally remove containers instead of cleaning them up.

(There is an argument for just using 'podman rm' instead of 'podman cleanup --rm', but cleanup does have different semantics given that we only ever expect it to run when the container has just exited. I think it might be useful to keep the two separate for things like 'podman events'...)

comments.body: LGTM in generaly, but would like other head nods and happy tests.

We're running into our old friend #1640 - I think I've made it worse by moving `--rm` to the cleanup process.

Might have an idea for how to fix this, actually...

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
A lot of the test failures our from our horrible abuse of the `--rm` flag in the test suite

Alright, so we accidentally fixed `podman create --rm` here. On master, `podman create --rm` succeeds (when we have code that should be erroring - there is code to blow up on this happening) and does nothing (we have no code to remove containers anywhere except `run.go` on master). With this patch, `create --rm` will successfully remove containers after they exit.

Unfortunately, our test suite used `create --rm` a lot, in ways that I'm really certain are not correct at all. I'm honestly not certain how these tests passes in the first place.
Alright, this last error is actually very concerning - the container is being removed while someone else holds its lock...
I can't reproduce locally, and there is no reason for that test to have --rm, so let's see if removing it gets us anywhere

Tests seem to be green, finally
@rhatdan @baude @TomSweeneyRedHat @vrothberg PTAL

One thought - this PR will allow `--detach` and `--rm` to be used together without issue.

Right now, we disable this.

Should we reenable it?
@mheon Reenable it in a different PR once this gets merged.
